messages.content: You are the Host Forensics Expert. Target OS: Windows. Vector Space: 4D windows_math. Source: Sysmon event stream. Schema: sysmon_event_id, Image, CommandLine, ParentImage, User, IntegrityLevel, TargetImage, GrantedAccess, TargetObject, Details, ImageLoaded, Signed, SignatureStatus, PipeName, QueryName, TargetFilename, TamperingType, Hashes. Identify Living-off-the-Land (LOLBAS) tradecraft. Output MITRE ATT&CK + containment.

Spatial Anomaly Detected.
Source: sysmon_sensor
Vector: <|spatial_vector|>
Windows Host Telemetry — mshta.exe LOLBAS Proxy Execution.
Host: SRV-97  User: alee
  EventID=1 (Process Create)
    Image: mshta.exe  ParentImage: explorer.exe
    CommandLine: mshta.exe C:\Users\tmorgan\AppData\Local\Temp\fdecac.hta
    (local HTA from user-writable path)
  EventID=3 (Network Connection) [if URL-based]
    Image: mshta.exe  DestinationIp=194.165.183.147  DestinationPort=80
  EventID=1 (Process Create — child)
    Image: powershell.exe  ParentImage: mshta.exe
    payload_delivered=YES  mshta_spawned_shell=YES

<analysis>
[AXIS 1] Benign Alternative Assessment:
  mshta.exe is occasionally used for legitimate HTML Application deployment in enterprise environments. However, legitimate HTA apps are stored in Program Files, are signed by IT, and never spawn cmd.exe or powershell.exe.
[AXIS 2] Behavioral Proof Assessment:
  mshta.exe spawning powershell.exe (shell interpreter) is not a legitimate HTA application behavior. local HTA from user-writable path. Parent explorer.exe has no operational reason to invoke mshta.exe for execution. EventID 3 outbound connection from mshta.exe = downloading payload, not rendering a static HTA.
[AXIS 3] Entity Coverage:
  Host SRV-97 (alee): mshta.exe used as binary proxy to execute arbitrary code via powershell.exe child process. No disk artifact required.
[CONCLUSION] mshta.exe LOLBAS proxy execution confirmed — spawning powershell.exe from explorer.exe.
</analysis>
TRUE POSITIVE. MITRE T1218.005 (System Binary Proxy Execution: Mshta). Kill mshta.exe and powershell.exe, isolate host, trace parent explorer.exe for initial access.
RECOMMENDED_ACTION: contain

Spatial Anomaly Detected.
Source: sysmon_sensor
Vector: <|spatial_vector|>
Windows Host Telemetry — Legitimate HTA Administrative Tool.
  EventID=1  Image: mshta.exe  ParentImage: explorer.exe
    CommandLine: mshta.exe C:\Program Files\ITTools\UpdateManager.hta
    code_signed=YES  vendor=corp-pki-ca
  no_child_shell_process=YES  no_network_connection=YES
  cmdb_registered=YES  change_ticket=CHG-61145

<analysis>
[AXIS 1] Benign Alternative Assessment:
  IT-deployed signed HTA tool from Program Files — no shell child, no network, corporate PKI signature, CMDB-registered.
[AXIS 2] Behavioral Proof Assessment:
  CommandLine points to Program Files (not temp). Signed by corp PKI. No cmd.exe/powershell.exe child. No outbound connection. CMDB-registered.
[AXIS 3] Entity Coverage:
  Authorized IT HTA tool — signed, Program Files path, no shell spawn.
[CONCLUSION] IT HTA tool UpdateManager.hta — signed, no child shell, no network.
</analysis>
FALSE POSITIVE. T1218.005 — AUTHORIZED HTA TOOL. No action.
RECOMMENDED_ACTION: dismiss
